title: A man and his wife go home to her parents; Hays killed in shoot-out with Sly; man killed by a live wire as he shot out insulators
description: A man's wife goes home to her parents, and he goes too. Hays killed in shoot-out with Sly. Hays unpopular, but honest, fearless, and enforced the law. Hays' sons no good. Joe Wells escapes from Hays because he can t see him in the dark. A man killed by a live wire as he shot out insulators. Maybe Mrs. Sly shot Hays; maybe he deserved to get shot. Moonshine killed a "nigger" (Joe).

title: Noise of trains in Troy; building railroad cut by Troy; rise and fall of Denver, near Grangeville
description: Noise of trains in Troy. Building railroad cut (by Troy). The rise and fall of Denver, near Grangeville, by-passed by the railroad. Camas Prairie around Grangeville very much like Latah County country. Severity of winter near here. Clay soil on Camas Prairie. How the Rowans met.

title: The stabbing of Driscoll; recent killing of a troublemaker by a policeman from Stites
description: The stabbing of Driscoll, who was bothering a girl, by Shoemaker. Was Driscoll drawing a gun? Recent killing of a troublemaker by a policeman from Stites, who resigned his jpb first.

title: Killing of Clemm in Troy
description: Killing of Clemm in Troy, in a restaurant owned by two blacks. Trouble was caused by local people much more than by transient lumberjacks.

title: Lumberjacks honest but drank earnings; repaying loans
description: Lumberjacks honest but drank up their earnings, Repaying loans in Grand Forks, Canada. Jacks were mostly single.

title: Grangeville man shot full of led in his cell after ignoring warnings to stop beating his wife
description: A Grangeville man, part Mexican, was shot full of lead in his cell, after ignoring men's warnings to stop beating his wife. Twenty men fired one bullet apiece into him. Frank's friend, in the next cell for cattle rustling, was very scared.

title: Mrs. Rowan's father signs petition to get man out of jail for stealing his horse and many others; eventually became one of the local aristocrats
description: Mrs. Rowan's father signs a petition to get a man out of jail. He'd stolen his horse and many others, although his family denied it, and he eventually became one of the aristocrats of the area.

title: Man trying to get out of the country on horseback, caught and lynched near Whitebird
description: A man trying to get out of the country on horseback, caught and lynched near Whitebird in someone's front yard. Mrs. Rowan thought that hiding him would have been better.

title: As a boy Frank meets a man who had just gone crazy; Troy man had been institutionalized and threatens Frank with weapons, but it only kidding
description: As a boy Frank meets a man who had just gone crazy. At Troy a man who had been institutionalized threatens Frank with weapons, but is only kidding.

title: Burying a stranger found hanging in Camp's Canyon near Burnt Ridge; body was exhumed and disappeared
description: Burying a stranger found hanging in Camp's Canyon, near Burnt Ridge. The body was exhumed and disappeared. No one said who he was. A man killed in a cabin on Burnt Ridge.

title: Why O.K. Olson built his home in the canyon (a joke). O.K. pretends to be mad
description: Why O.K. Olson built his home in the canyon (a joke). O.K. pretends to be mad.

title: Per Johanson started the brick yard in Troy, never spoke English; manager who put in the kilns had to put the business in debt $7,500; Watson new Scotch manager
description: Per Johanson, who started the brick yard in Troy, never spoke English. The manager who put in the kilns had to put the business in debt $7,500, and was replaced. Watson, the new Scotch manager, is tracked down by his son, with Tom Christie's help, (continued)

title: Watson had left his wife in the old country, but when the son found him he forgot about his mother; Frank stands up to Watson
description: Watson had left his wife in the old country, but when the son found him he forgot about his mother. Frank stands up to Watson, and gets along with him. Sundquist talks back to Watson too.

title: Troy banks; trying to find out if a man had money
description: Troy banks. Trying to find out if a man had money. Ole and Axel Bohman started the policies Frank Brocke carried on. First bank robbery in Troy; how the robber got caught; Frank Brocke later loaned him money.

title: Man who burnt saloon down in Troy later was sheriff in Culdesac
description: Man who burnt saloon down in Troy later was sheriff in Culdesac.

title: A bucker gets himself fired by T.P. Jones, so he could gyppo; IWW conditions in camps
description: A bucker gets himself fired by T.P. Jones, so he could gyppo. IWW improved conditions in the camps. Wobblies blamed for much they didn't do, like a bad fire at Harrison, when the governor handed the people $100 to start rebuilding. A logger scared by a cop with a gun. Dick Ferrell couldn't preach.

title: The quality of cooking in the camps depended on standards of the foreman
description: The quality of the cooking in camps depended on the standards of the foreman. Frank cooked in camp, and was told he'd killed off a whole crew. He cooks birds that aren't clean.

title: Big Gil's pet bear; hanging the pet bear in Troy; pet billy goat
description: Big Gil's pet bear. Hanging a pet bear in Troy. A pet billy goat followed people; his throat gets cut. (continued)

title: Watching deer near Troy; Orvil Carlson's black angus is shot for a bear
description: Watching deer near Troy; a cat and deer who are friends. Orvil Carlson's black angus is shot for a bear. A bear after lard at the Rowan's place.

title: Grandma Friske insists that Rowan's daughter get sugar from her cupboard; she can's speak English; her son gets a housekeeper who can's speak Swedish
description: Grandma Friske insists that the Rowan's daughter get sugar from her cupboard. She can't speak English, and tries to borrow green onions from the Rowans. Her son gets a housekeeper who can't speak Swedish. Her death.

title: Turpentine and lard for chest colds; tuberculosis among relatives
description: Turpentine and lard for chest colds, Vicks fur burns. Tuberculosis among relatives. Daughter's husband "appeared" to her after death. Her own dead child "appeared" to Mrs. Rowan when she was sick recently, and delirious; the girl died of heart disease.

title: Spencer cemetery started because the price of a lot was too high; locating graves in the cemetery with a rod, marking them with fire brick
description: The Spencer cemetery was started because the price of a lot was too high. Locating graves in the cemetery with a rod, and marking them with fire brick.

title: Jealousy of some people about success. A burial, a birth and a death in one morning on Burnt Ridge
description: Jealousy of some people about success. A burial, a birth and a death in one morning on Burnt Ridge.

title: Wild Davey told wild stories and got by by "working people"; Irish still had to fight in an Irish town
description: Wild Davey told wild stories, and got by by "working people". A foolish boy who isn't so foolish. Good fighting in the early days; Irish had to fight in an Irish town.

title: A husband who didn't want to bring a baby to church
description: A husband who didn't want to bring a baby to church,

title: Falling stove pipe scares a family; visiting with the Native American women as they picked camas and cous
description: A falling stove pipe scares a family, who think it's Indians. Visiting with the Indian women, as they picked camas and cous. Watching her daughter on a train ride; the daughter mistakes a sleeping Indian for her aunt.

title: Leonard Marks became a hermit after his wife died; Hook Anderson had one arm, was a good working lumberjack
description: Leonard Marks became a hermit after his wife died. He is seen walking around his place without any clothes on. Hook Anderson had one arm, was a good working lumberjack.

title: The road work program in Troy during the Depression; Troy gets extra men because Genesee can's use all theirs; roads that wouldn't have been worked for many years except for depression
description: The road work program in Troy during the Depression. Troy gets extra men because Genesee can't use all theirs. Roads that wouldn't have been worked for many years except for depression. Foolish mistake in current road building by Nora.
